{"step_by_step_thinking": "The patient is a 12-year-old boy who presented with gross hematuria 2 days after an upper respiratory tract infection. The presence of 80% dysmorphic RBCs suggests an immune-mediated process. Among the given options, Henoch-Schönlein purpura (H.S. purpura) is the most likely diagnosis because it is an immune-mediated glomerulonephritis that often follows an upper respiratory tract infection in children. Microangiopathic thrombotic anaemia, IgA nephropathy, and post-streptococcal glomerulonephritis (PSGN) do not typically present with such a high percentage of dysmorphic RBCs.", "answer_choice": "D"}
